title: Frontend-Backend Communication Architecture — Comprehensive Cartography
summary: Complete map of how packages/app/ communicates with packages/opencode/ across 4 channels — SDK client, SSE event stream, WebSocket, Electron IPC.

sections.heading: 1. Entry Points & Dependency Chain
content: packages/app (SolidJS frontend): entry.tsx → app.tsx → AppBaseProviders → AppInterface → router → page components
packages/opencode (backend server): server.ts → HttpApiApp → route groups (global, session, instance, config, ...)
packages/sdk/js (shared SDK): auto-generated @hey-api/openapi-ts client wrapping fetch(). Published as @opencode-ai/sdk
packages/app/package.json depends on: @opencode-ai/sdk (workspace), @opencode-ai/core (workspace), @opencode-ai/ui (workspace), @tanstack/solid-query

sections.heading: 2. Communication Channel: HTTP REST via SDK Client
content: SDK creation chain: entry.tsx → ServerConnection.Http (URL + Basic auth) → createSdkForServer() → createOpencodeClient()
createOpencodeClient() in packages/sdk/js/src/v2/client.ts wraps fetch(), injects x-opencode-directory header, rewrites headers to query params for GET/HEAD
All SDK calls go through @tanstack/solid-query (useQuery, useMutation, useQueries)

Key SDK client methods used by the frontend: Global.health() GET /global/health, Global.event() SSE /global/event, Global.config.*, Session.* (list, get, create, prompt, command, delete, fork, revert, permissions), Instance.* (path, vcs, agent, config), Provider.*, File.*, Find.*, Mcp.*, Lsp.*, Pty.*, Command.*
SDK regenerated from OpenAPI spec via packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts

sections.heading: 3. Communication Channel: Server-Sent Events (SSE) — The Real-Time Backbone
content: Primary real-time channel. Managed by packages/app/src/context/server-sdk.tsx (291 lines)
Endpoint: GET /global/event with Content-Type: text/event-stream (defined in packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/global.ts:56-63)
Event type schema: GlobalEventSchema = { directory, project?, workspace?, payload: Union of BusEvent + SyncEvent }
SDK abstraction: packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/core/serverSentEvents.gen.ts parses data:, event:, id:, retry: fields from fetch() ReadableStream
Event coalescing: duplicate events coalesced by composite keys (session.status:dir:id, message.part.updated:dir:mid:pid)
Batching: events queued in memory, flushed via setTimeout every 16ms inside SolidJS batch() call
Delta tracking: when message.part.updated arrives after message.part.delta, stale delta is skipped
Heartbeat: 15s timeout — if no event received, aborts and reconnects (HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS=15000,RECONNECT_DELAY_MS=250)
Visibility handling: on document.visibilitychange → hidden → visible, reconnects if heartbeat stale
Each SSE attempt has its own AbortController for clean cancellation

sections.heading: 4. Communication Channel: WebSocket (Terminal PTY Only)
content: Only WebSocket usage is for terminal I/O — nowhere else in the frontend uses WebSocket
URL pattern: ws[s]://host/pty/:id/connect with directory, cursor, auth params (packages/app/src/utils/terminal-websocket-url.ts)
PTY lifecycle: POST /pty to create → WebSocket connect → SSE event pty.exited on close → DELETE /pty
No @solid-primitives/websocket found — WebSocket is managed manually

sections.heading: 5. Communication Channel: Electron IPC Bridge (window.api)
content: Desktop-only. Electron preload sets window.api object
Fallback: packages/app/src/browser-api.ts provides no-op polyfill for browser mode

API surface: setTitlebar, exportDebugLogs, getCustomAgents, getMcpServers, *GitStatus, *DependencyStatus, *SessionMemory, *TestStatus, *PullRequestStatus, showNotification, store* (Get/Set/Delete/Clear/Keys), getWindowConfig, locale, deep link handling, menu commands, OAuth callbacks
Used by ambient watchers (packages/app/src/ambient/watchers/): git-status, session-memory, test-status, deps, pr-activity
When window.api is unavailable, watchers fall back to HTTP fetch() to /api/{domain}/status endpoints with short timeouts (3-5s)
Polling intervals: git 30s, tests 60s, deps 300s, PR 120s, memory 60s (packages/app/src/context/ambient.tsx)

sections.heading: 6. State Management Architecture
content: Three-layer state: (1) Global stores via createStore + TanStack Query (config, projects, providers), (2) Per-directory child stores via createChildStoreManager (sessions, messages, permissions, questions, VCS), (3) Session view state via persisted createStore (tabs, scroll, terminal)
Context hierarchy: PlatformProvider → LanguageProvider → SettingsProvider → ServerProvider → ServerSDKProvider → ServerSyncProvider → SDKProvider + ...
ServerProvider: manages multiple ServerConnection types — Http (remote), Sidecar (local desktop, base or WSL), Ssh (SSH tunnel), persisted to localStorage
ServerSDKProvider: creates SSE stream + SDK client per server
ServerSyncProvider: orchestrates bootstrap + event routing + TanStack Query
Per-directory: lazy-created child stores, max 30 dirs, 20min idle TTL, reference-counted pinning
Optimistic updates: messages added before server confirmation, tracked via OptimisticItem[], merged on SSE event confirmation

sections.heading: 7. HTTP API Route Groups (Full Surface)
content: Global: GET /global/health, GET /global/event (SSE), GET/PATCH /global/config, POST /global/dispose, POST /global/upgrade

Session: GET /session (list, status, get, children, todo, diff, messages), GET /session/:id/message, POST /session (create, import, fork, abort, init, share, summarize, prompt, promptAsync, command, shell, revert, unrevert, permissionRespond), DELETE /session/:id (remove, deleteMessage, deletePart), PATCH (update, updatePart)
Instance: POST /instance/dispose, GET /instance/(path, vcs, vcsStatus, vcsDiff, vcsDiffRaw, command, agent, skill, lsp, formatter), POST /instance/vcsApply
Config: GET/PATCH /config, GET /config/providers
Provider: GET /provider, GET /provider/auth, POST /provider/:id/oauth/authorize, POST /provider/:id/oauth/callback
V2: GET /api/session, POST /api/session/:id/prompt, GET /api/model, GET /api/provider, GET /api/provider/:id
Experimental: GET /experimental/(console, consoleOrgs, tool, toolIDs, worktree, session, resource), POST /experimental/(consoleSwitch, worktreeCreate, worktreeReset), DELETE /experimental/worktree

TUI Control: POST /tui/(appendPrompt, openHelp, openSessions, openThemes, openModels, submitPrompt, clearPrompt, executeCommand, showToast, publish, selectSession, controlResponse), GET /tui/controlNext
Sync (experimental): POST /sync/(start, replay, steal, history)
Event: GET /event (SSE, per-instance)
Permission: GET /permission, POST /permission/:requestID/reply
Question: GET /question, POST /question/:requestID/(reply, reject)
PTY: GET /pty/shells, GET /pty/list
Auth: PUT /auth, DELETE /auth
Plus: file/*, find/*, mcp/*, lsp/*, command/*, formatter/*, log

sections.heading: 8. SSE Event Flow & Event Types
content: SSE stream → server-sdk.tsx (coalesce/batch via GlobalEmitter) → server-sync.tsx (event-reducer) → SolidJS stores
Global events handled by applyGlobalEvent(): global.disposed, server.connected, project.updated

Directory events handled by applyDirectoryEvent() (18+ types): session created/updated/deleted, session.diff, todo.updated, session.status, message updated/removed/part/delta, permission asked/replied, question asked/replied/rejected, vcs.branch.updated, lsp.updated, server.instance.disposed
Inspector subscribes to ALL events for debug visualization (capped at 10000 events)
Session-viz subscribes to: standard SDK events + coordination events (heartbeat, task_status, path_claimed, activity_logged)

sections.heading: 9. TanStack Query Patterns
content: QueryClient at root via packages/app/src/app.tsx: QueryProvider wrapper
bootstrap phase: bootstrapGlobal() runs 4 parallel queries (config, providers, path, projects) via Promise.allSettled
Per-directory bootstrap: up to 12 parallel queries (agents, config, sessions, project, VCS, commands, permissions, questions, MCP, LSP, providers)
Key query keys: [bootstrap], [config], [project], [directory, path], [directory, providers], [directory, agents], [directory, mcp], [directory, lsp], [null, providers], [directory, loadSessions]
Helpers: useQuery, useQueries, useMutation, useQueryClient, queryOptions, useIsFetching
Config update mutation invalidates all provider queries to surface newly configured providers

sections.heading: 10. Connection Resilience & Recovery
content: SSE auto-reconnect: 250ms delay after any failure
Heartbeat: 15s timeout aborts and reconnects the SSE stream
Visibility change: on tab refocus, checks last event time and reconnects if stale
Each reconnect attempt has a fresh AbortController
Refresh queue (global-sync/queue.ts): processes 2 directory refreshes at a time with backpressure
Session load caching: prefetch cache (15s TTL), versioned cache invalidation
Error handling: formatServerError utility, toast notifications for bootstrap failures

key_files.role: SSE event stream engine — coalesce, batch, reconnect, heartbeat
Global state orchestrator — bootstrap, event routing, TanStack Query
Server connection management (HTTP, sidecar, SSH, WSL)
Optimistic sync layer for session messages
SSE event → SolidJS store state mutations
Lazy per-directory stores with LRU eviction
createSdkForServer — SDK client factory with auth
createOpencodeClient — SDK client with header rewriting
Global API: health, event (SSE), config, dispose, upgrade
All-event inspector with categorization and filtering
App entry — server URL resolution, platform initialization

patterns.description: A single persistent SSE connection drives all real-time state changes. Events are coalesced, batched (16ms frames), and dispatched to SolidJS stores via event reducers.
All state is organized as SolidJS contexts created via @opencode-ai/ui/context's createSimpleContext utility (useXxx() + XxxProvider).

patterns.pattern: Per-Directory Child Stores with LRU Eviction
patterns.description: Each project directory gets an isolated state store (lazy, max 30, 20min idle TTL, reference-counted pinning).
Messages added locally before server confirmation, merged via mergeOptimisticPage. @opencode-ai/core/util/binary search for sorted arrays.
All HTTP REST calls go through @tanstack/solid-query for deduplication, caching, and invalidation.
HTTP (remote server), Sidecar (local desktop/WSL), SSH (SSH tunnel with HTTP proxy). Discriminated union in ServerConnection namespace.
